events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: ABS-to-PVC glued with wrong cement. This is a non-compliant material transition. ABS and PVC require specific transition cement (ABS-to-PVC transition cement) or proper mechanical transition. Using wrong cement (e.g., regular PVC cement on ABS, or ABS cement on PVC) creates a defective joint that won't bond properly. IPC requires proper joining methods per manufacturer instructions and approved standards. This is a fail.

    "Cut out and replace the non-compliant ABS-to-PVC joint",

  "rationale": "ABS and PVC are dissimilar plastics and cannot be reliably joined with standard PVC or ABS cement. IPC and manufacturer listings require an approved transition method (listed ABS-to-PVC transition cement or mechanical fitting). Use of the wrong cement produces an installed-defective, non-compliant joint that must be corrected before close-out.",
